The FINDINGS.


"Charge /, contained in the
charges filed by Mr.. Elliott before
this council, has been sustained.


Charge 2, filed in the charges
by Mr.. Elliott, has been sustained.


Charge is as follows; We, up
on investigation, find that there
was a conspiracy, but that there is
no evidence to show that Mr.. Sui
livan was in any way connected,
and therefore exonerate Mr.. Sulli
Won on this charge


Charge 4. We find that while
Mr.. Sullivan did refuse the Spo-
kane Press access to the police
blotter, he did so upon the advice
and opinion of the corporation
counsel, and therefore find this
charge not sustained. However.
that if the police blotter is open
to inspection to one or any paper
it should be open to all.


Charge 5.-Protecting houses of
ill-fame. We find that while the
same is sustained by the evidence,
it could not have existed except
and alone by the knowledge and
consent of the board of police COM
miSSioneTs and the mayor, and
further find from the evidence ad-
duced that since the filing Of said
charge these houses have been
closed.


Appointment Rules Violated.


Charge 6. Favoritism in ap-
pointments on police force. We
find that the civil service rules
governing the regulations of the
police department have been gross
ly violated and, while Mr.. Sullivan
made the recommendations, the
appointing powers are vested in
the police commissioners who made
the appointments on his recon-
mendation, and therefore, the
charges as to Mr.. Sullivan are not
sustained.


Charge Z. We find that after
the filing of these charges Mr..
Sullivan has resigned from the Of
flee of acting chief, and further
find that the mayor, through his
appointment of Sullivan as acting
chief for about 16 months, disre-
garded the provisions of the city
charter requiring him to appoint a
chief of police, and should be held
equally responsible .or any and all
acts of the office during that
period.


Wanted Captain to Appear.


charge s. From the fact that
the chief of police refused to make
defense in the case when called
upon to do so by the council, we
can see no other way than to sus-
tain charge 8.


Whereas, During the hearing of
these charges against Sullivan the
testimony seemingly developed
charges against Ol4lcers Hogan and
Downey and other unknown Off
GERS that they received money
from prostitute sfor protection of
such person or persons to ply their
unlawful business;


Now, therefore, we recommend
= to the police commissioners to
tmake a careful investigation of
Isaid charges at once and make their
. report thereof on the same and file
= the same with the city council, and
from date hereof recommend the
said Officers Hogan and Downey
